meta.study: A6 reliability study — parts i (synthesis) + ii (coverage)

technicalCompetencies.statement: Students build a professional LinkedIn profile with standard employer-facing sections and networking etiquette.
Students engage employers at career events and capture accurate takeaways about company roles, business focus, and opportunities.
Students participate in career-development services such as resume review, mock interviews, networking, and related career-center programming.
Students calculate personal living costs and salary requirements using a budgeting spreadsheet with formulas and expense records.
Students verify degree progress and organize a forward-looking academic course plan in CourseLineup.
Students investigate graphic communications career paths and connect employer information to possible roles in the field.

technicalCompetencies.statement: Students analyze packaging requirements and identify required information, package functions, and market-positioning features on real package examples.
Students develop brand systems for package and specialty-print product lines using logos, colors, fonts, market research, and product positioning.
Students build and prepress flexographic test targets for process calibration and printability evaluation.
Students prepare flexographic prepress files by making and checking trapping, separations, screening, angles, and barcode decisions for production.
Students design and produce functional and promotional flexographic labels that align brand intent with production constraints.
Students analyze flexographic print quality, defects, and process-control results using qualitative observation and quantitative data.
Students document packaging and print-production workflows in technical reports that explain decisions, methods, results, and improvements.
Students develop paperboard package structures and artwork that are shelf-ready, branded, and commercially usable.
Students prepare and produce specialty-print products by selecting appropriate processes, artwork strategies, and production considerations for the chosen application.
Students communicate package and print-production work through organized technical writing and oral presentation using domain-specific language.
Students analyze packaging requirements and identify required information, package functions, and market-positioning features on real package examples.

matchedPairs.statement_a: Students communicate package and print-production work through organized technical writing and oral presentation using domain-specific language.
matchedPairs.statement_b: Students communicate package and print-production work through organized technical writing and oral presentation using domain-specific language.

matchedPairs.statement_a: Students analyze packaging requirements and identify required information, package functions, and market-positioning features on real package examples.
matchedPairs.statement_b: Students analyze packaging requirements and identify required information, package functions, and market-positioning features on real package examples.

matchedPairs.statement_a: Students develop brand systems for package and specialty-print product lines using logos, colors, fonts, market research, and product positioning.
matchedPairs.statement_b: Students develop brand systems for package and specialty-print product lines using logos, colors, fonts, market research, and product positioning.

matchedPairs.statement_a: Students prepare flexographic prepress files by making and checking trapping, separations, screening, angle, and barcode decisions for production.
matchedPairs.statement_b: Students prepare flexographic prepress files by making and checking trapping, separations, screening, angle, and barcode decisions for production.

matchedPairs.statement_a: Students build and prepress flexographic test targets for process calibration and printability evaluation.
matchedPairs.statement_b: Students build and prepress flexographic test targets for process calibration and printability evaluation.

matchedPairs.statement_a: Students design and produce functional and promotional flexographic labels that align brand intent with production constraints.
matchedPairs.statement_b: Students design and produce functional and promotional flexographic labels that align brand intent with production constraints.

matchedPairs.statement_a: Students analyze flexographic print quality, defects, and process-control results using qualitative observation and quantitative data.
matchedPairs.statement_b: Students analyze flexographic print quality, defects, and process-control results using qualitative observation and quantitative data.

matchedPairs.statement_a: Students document packaging and print-production workflows in technical reports that explain decisions, methods, results, and improvements.
matchedPairs.statement_b: Students document packaging and print-production workflows in technical reports that explain decisions, methods, results, and improvements.

matchedPairs.statement_a: Students develop paperboard package structures and artwork that are shelf-ready, branded, and commercially usable.
matchedPairs.statement_b: Students develop paperboard package structures and artwork that are shelf-ready, branded, and commercially usable.

matchedPairs.statement_a: Students prepare and produce specialty-print products by selecting appropriate processes, artwork strategies, and production considerations for the chosen application.
matchedPairs.statement_b: Students prepare and produce specialty-print products by selecting appropriate processes, artwork strategies, and production considerations for the chosen application.

matchedPairs.statement_a: Students communicate package and print-production work through organized technical writing and oral presentation using domain-specific language.
matchedPairs.statement_b: Students communicate package and print-production work through organized technical writing and oral presentation using domain-specific language.

technicalCompetencies.statement: Students measure printed color and material samples with spectrophotometers using correct industry settings and record Lab data accurately.
Students calculate and interpret color-difference values against standards using Delta E formulas and tolerances.
Students formulate inks to target colors with software tools, measure results, and iteratively adjust formulations until tolerance is met.
Students test and analyze physical and optical properties of inks through drawdowns, measurement, and formal lab-report interpretation.
Students test substrate properties, document observations and measured results, and compare characteristics across samples.
Students create and apply ICC-based color-management workflows to characterize device output and manage RGB-to-CMYK reproduction.
Students produce and evaluate proofs through visual comparison and standards-based control-wedge analysis against press output.
Students create and use CxF spectral data to support spot-color proofing workflows.
Students document measurement procedures, settings, calculations, and findings in Excel templates, charts, graphs, and formal technical reports.
Students analyze brand-color reproduction across inks, substrates, and output systems in a capstone technical report.
